---
title: "Yuda Bi"
subtitle: |
  Ph.D. in Computer Science \
  [TReNDS Center](https://trendscenter.org/) (Georgia State University, Georgia Tech, Emory)
image: files/profile/avatar.jpg
keywords: [Yuda Bi, statistical physics, information geometry, theoretical neuroscience, spectral graph theory, nonequilibrium thermodynamics, TReNDS Center, Georgia State University]
page-layout: full
toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 14em
  image-alt: "Portrait photo of Yuda Bi"
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:ybi3@gsu.edu
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=yclnpogAAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/yudabitrends
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yudabi/

listing:
  - id: recent-notes
    contents: notes
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
    fields: [date, title, description]
    sort: "date desc"
---

## About

I received my Ph.D. from the [TReNDS Center](https://trendscenter.org/) (GSU, Georgia Tech, Emory), advised by [Prof. Vince D. Calhoun](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=WNOoGKIAAAAJ).

In 2025 I pivoted from engineering-driven multimodal neuroimaging toward a **theory-first** research program grounded in statistical physics, nonequilibrium thermodynamics, and information geometry.

### Research Directions

[Statistical Physics]{.research-tag} [Information Geometry]{.research-tag} [Nonequilibrium Thermodynamics]{.research-tag} [Theoretical Neuroscience]{.research-tag} [Spectral Graph Theory]{.research-tag} [Financial Physics]{.research-tag} [Structural Biology]{.research-tag}

### Education

::: {.education-item}
**Ph.D. in Computer Science**, Georgia State University, 2020–2025
:::

::: {.education-item}
**M.S. in Computer Science**, University of Georgia, 2017–2020
:::

::: {.education-item}
**B.S. in Management**, Shandong University of Science and Technology, 2013–2017
:::

---

::: {.stats-grid}
::: {.stat-card}
[7]{.stat-number}
[Under Review]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[4]{.stat-number}
[PRL / PRE]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[3]{.stat-number}
[Journal Articles]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[5]{.stat-number}
[Conference Papers]{.stat-label}
:::
:::

---

::: {.theory-card}

#### [The Geometry of Invisible Forces: A Quartic Detection Theory](notes/2026-04-quartic-detection-theory/)

::: {.theory-eq}
$D_{\mathrm{KL}}^{\min}(\lambda) = C\,\lambda^4 + O(\lambda^6)$
:::

Hidden variables are orders of magnitude harder to detect than classical statistics predicts. When a reduced model absorbs the leading perturbation through reparametrization, only the *normal residual* survives — producing a quartic, not quadratic, detection law. A single probe is provably blind; two probes sharing the hidden driver break the impossibility via cross-spectral geometry.

Four papers under review at *Physical Review Letters* and *Physical Review E*.

::: {.theory-links}
[Theory Note](notes/2026-04-quartic-detection-theory/){.btn-note}
[Project Page](projects.html#quartic-detection-theory-the-geometry-of-hidden-variables){.btn-project}
[Tutorial PDF](files/quartic_detection_tutorial.pdf){.btn-pdf}
:::

:::

::: {.motto}
"The sad truth is that most evil is done by people who never make up their minds to be good or evil."
[— Hannah Arendt]{.author}
:::

::: {.visitor-badge}
![Visitor count](https://visitor-badge.laobi.icu/badge?page_id=yudabitrends.github.io&left_color=gray&right_color=blue)
:::

---

## Recent Notes

:::{#recent-notes}
:::
